title: rootless has no http traffic without --net host
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

I have a problem getting traffic flowing via exposed port. I start podman run as user, and share port via ```-p 4000:4000```, but no http connection can be made, unless adding ```--net host```.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Git clone site and run jekyll:
```shell
git clone -b ldap-sync git@github.com:ikke-t/docs.git /tmp/docs
podman run --name jekyll --rm -it -v /tmp/docs:/site:z quay.io/hybridcloudpatterns/jekyll-container:latest
make serve
```
2. Verify from other shell:
```shell
curl http://localhost:4000
```


**Describe the results you received:**
```
curl: (7) Failed to connect to localhost port 4000 after 0 ms: Connection refused
```

**Describe the results you expected:**

web site to load


**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

Works always if added ```--net host```.

**Output of `podman version`:**

```

Version:      4.0.3
API Version:  4.0.3
Go Version:   go1.18
Built:        Fri Apr  1 21:21:54 2022
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

comments.body:
Hi @ikke-t, Thanks for creating the issue. 

Something looks wrong with the image `quay.io/hybridcloudpatterns/jekyll-container:latest` command fails for me with `/usr/bin/make: /usr/bin/make: cannot execute binary file`. I am unable to reproduce since image is not working for me.

It fails if I add the make serve to the end of podman line. But if start it without any command (entrypoint is bash), and while in container's bash, I can type ```make serve```. @flouthoc can you please try?

@ikke-t Its fails for me again, also I see no files at all the the entrypoint.
```console
podman run --name jekyll --rm -it -p 4000:4000 quay.io/hybridcloudpatterns/jekyll-container:latest
bash-5.1# make serve
make: *** No rule to make target 'serve'.  Stop.
```
ah, you didn't do the ```git clone``` part, nor did you do the ```-v``` part :D

that container is only having jekyll tools, which build the website volume mounted to it. Now you didn't clone the website to be mounted, nor gave it to jekyll  container. Please check the reproducal part for commands.

Could you try this, forwarding works just fine for me
```console
podman run -p 4000:4000 hashicorp/http-echo -listen=:4000 -text="hello world"
Resolved "hashicorp/http-echo" as an alias (/home/fl/.cache/containers/short-name-aliases.conf)
Trying to pull docker.io/hashicorp/http-echo:latest...
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob 86399148984b done  
Copying config a6838e9a6f done  
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
2022/05/11 11:19:24 Server is listening on :4000
2022/05/11 11:19:38 localhost:4000 _____:60656 "GET / HTTP/1.1" 200 12 "Mozilla/5.0 (X11; Fedora; Linux x86_64; rv:98.0) Gecko/20100101 Firefox/98.0" 7.743µs
2022/05/11 11:19:38 localhost:4000 ______:60656 "GET /favicon.ico HTTP/1.1" 200 12 "Mozilla/5.0 (X11; Fedora; Linux x86_64; rv:98.0) Gecko/20100101 Firefox/98.0" 14.427µs
```
that works. I also noticed in meanwhile that lighttpd works as well. I wonder why the ruby jekyll container requires net host?
got tip in podman chat the jekyll listens to 127.0.0.1 and thus won't get the traffic. I added ```--host 0.0.0.0```, and now it works! Don't really understand why, but so it seems to work ;D
